channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Get a month of 5G Home Internet on T-Mobile and $300 cash back - here's how
item.description: T-Mobile is offering a deal for new customers that includes a month of 5G Home Internet and $300 cash back. This promotion aims to attract more users to their internet service.

item.title: Adobe Creative Cloud Pro is now 50% off - how to get the deal
item.description: Adobe is currently offering a 50% discount on its Creative Cloud Pro subscription, which includes access to over 20 apps and generative AI tools. This deal makes professional creative software more accessible.

item.title: Burglar alarm biz burgled: ADT confirms cyber intrusion after ShinyHunters extortion attempt
item.description: ADT has confirmed a cyber intrusion after an extortion attempt by the ShinyHunters group, which claims to have stolen over 10 million records. The breach raises concerns about security in the home security industry.

item.title: Microsoft updates the Windows Update Experience: You can hit pause now
item.description: Microsoft has updated the Windows Update Experience, allowing users to pause updates indefinitely. This change addresses user frustrations with problematic updates that can disrupt device functionality.

item.title: You can still get a free Samsung Galaxy Watch 8 deal at T-Mobile - here's how to qualify
item.description: T-Mobile is offering a free Samsung Galaxy Watch 8 when customers add a new watch line to their mobile plan. This promotion also includes a discount on the Apple Watch Series 11.

item.title: I changed these 12+ Hisense TV settings to significantly improve the picture quality
item.description: Tweaking settings on a Hisense TV can significantly enhance picture quality, according to a recent guide. This advice helps users optimize their viewing experience without needing to purchase new equipment.

item.title: This bestselling gaming device is not a Nintendo or a PlayStation - and I highly recommend it
item.description: The Nex Playground, a gaming device that encourages physical activity in children, is currently on sale. This device stands out as a popular alternative to traditional gaming consoles.

item.title: In the beginning was the Bork: 'Heart of the Earth' exhibit reveals Raspberry Pi in existential crisis
item.description: An exhibit titled 'Heart of the Earth' features a Raspberry Pi embedded in ancient rock, symbolizing a blend of technology and nature. This installation raises questions about the intersection of digital and physical worlds.

item.title: I stress-tested this SSD enclosure with a bulldozer - here's how it held up
item.description: The Terramaster D1 SSD enclosure has been stress-tested for durability and performance, proving it can withstand extreme conditions. This product is designed for both speed and resilience.

item.title: Meta inks deal for solar power at night, beamed from space
item.description: Meta has signed a contract with Overview Energy for solar power beamed from space, marking a step towards innovative energy solutions. This initiative could revolutionize how solar energy is harnessed.

item.title: ICO chief John Edwards steps back as workplace probe quietly unfolds
item.description: John Edwards, the chief of the UK's data watchdog, has stepped back from his role amid an ongoing HR investigation. This absence raises concerns about leadership stability at the Information Commissioner's Office.

item.title: Watch out UK taxpayers: 28,000 HMRC staffers just got an AI copilot
item.description: HMRC is implementing Microsoft Copilot for 28,000 staff after a trial showed it saved each employee about 26 minutes daily. This move signifies a major investment in AI to enhance productivity in government operations.

item.title: Anthropic's magic code-sniffer: More Swiss cheese than cheddar, for now
item.description: Anthropic's AI code security tool, Mythos, is currently limited in effectiveness, revealing vulnerabilities it was trained to detect. This raises questions about the reliability of AI in cybersecurity.

item.title: The AI-designed car is taking shape
item.description: The automotive industry is beginning to integrate AI into car design, moving beyond traditional sketches to advanced 3D visualization and VR tools. This shift could revolutionize how vehicles are conceptualized and produced.

item.title: evalguard-sdk added to PyPI
item.description: The EvalGuard SDK has been added to PyPI, serving as a key tool for teams developing LLM agents. This addition enhances the resources available for AI development.

item.title: DeepSeek launches 1.6 trillion parameter V4 on Huawei chips as U.S. escalates AI theft accusations — U.S. gov't alleges IP theft by DeepSeek and other Chinese AI firms
item.description: DeepSeek has unveiled its V4 large language model, which boasts 1.6 trillion parameters, amidst U.S. allegations of intellectual property theft against Chinese AI firms. This development highlights the competitive landscape in AI technology.

item.title: Devious New AI Tool “Clones” Software So That the Original Creator Doesn’t Hold a Copyright Over the New Version
item.description: A new AI tool has emerged that can clone software, potentially bypassing copyright protections for original creators. This raises significant legal and ethical concerns in the software industry.

item.title: Brockman’s diary called it a lie. Now a jury will hear it.
item.description: Jury selection has begun for a trial that will examine whether OpenAI's transition from a nonprofit to a for-profit entity violated charitable trust laws. This case could have major implications for the future of nonprofit tech organizations.

item.title: zeno-provider-claude 1.0.1
item.description: The Zeno platform has released version 1.0.1 of the Claude Agent SDK, which includes sub-agents and built-in tools for enhanced functionality. This update aims to improve user experience in AI applications.

item.title: zeno-provider-openai 1.0.1
item.description: Zeno has introduced version 1.0.1 of its OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions provider, expanding options for developers using various AI models. This update reflects the ongoing evolution of AI integration.

item.title: concinno 4.0.0
item.description: Concinno 4.0.0 introduces AI behavior governance features for Claude Code, including input rewriting and a safety agent, enhancing AI reliability and safety.

item.title: llm-notebook 1.3.29
item.description: The llm-notebook 1.3.29 offers a Karpathy-style LLM wiki, integrating Claude Code with Codex CLI and Obsidian for improved documentation and coding.

item.title: ciagen 1.0.1
item.description: Ciagen 1.0.1 presents a Controllable Image Augmentation framework that utilizes Stable Diffusion and ControlNet for enhanced image processing.

item.title: OpenClaw Hardware Requirements
item.description: The OpenClaw Hardware Requirements guide reveals that running the popular open-source AI agent framework effectively requires more than the stated minimum of 4GB RAM.

item.title: gemcode 0.3.115
item.description: Gemcode 0.3.115 is a local-first coding agent designed for Google Gemini, enhancing coding efficiency and collaboration.

item.title: Game Theory, Fishery Improvement Projects, OpenAI, More: Sunday ResearchBuzz, April 26, 2026
item.description: A new resource on game theory has been launched, offering an interactive course via Wolfram Language to help users dominate competition.

item.title: Why did OpenAI apologize to Tumbler Ridge? #tech
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman apologized to Tumbler Ridge after failing to notify law enforcement about a potential mass shooting suspect, raising concerns about AI ethics.

item.title: The Fully Non-Human Web: No One Builds The Page, No One Visits It via @sejournal, @slobodanmanic
item.description: The article discusses the emergence of a 'Fully Non-Human Web,' where AI manages transactional systems, impacting how brands approach visibility and trust.

item.title: I'm a Lifelong Windows User. These 6 macOS 27 Upgrades Could Change My Mind
item.description: An article compares macOS upgrades to Windows, suggesting that recent improvements in MacBook could sway lifelong Windows users to switch.

item.title: I took my character animation to a whole new level with the Acer Predator Helios Neo 16S AI
item.description: The Acer Predator Helios Neo 16S AI laptop showcases impressive processing power for character animation, despite its GPU limitations.

item.title: Sunday: Hili dialogue
item.description: The Turkmen Racing Horse Festival is set for April 26, 2026, marking an important cultural event. This annual festival showcases Turkmenistan's rich equestrian heritage.

item.title: quantization-util added to PyPI
item.description: A new tool called quantization-util has been added to PyPI, allowing users to convert small HuggingFace models into various formats. This enhances model efficiency and accessibility.

item.title: mnemostack 0.2.0a6
item.description: The release of mnemostack 0.2.0a6 introduces a memory stack for AI agents, integrating advanced retrieval techniques and an inference layer. This development aims to enhance AI memory and recall capabilities.

item.title: torch-ans 0.1.3
item.description: The torch-ans 0.1.3 extension for PyTorch has been released, focusing on ANS-based compression using C++/CUDA. This tool aims to optimize data handling in AI applications.

item.title: Privacy Advocate Accuses US Government of Investing in AI-Powered Mass Surveillance
item.description: Privacy advocate Anne Toomey McKenna warns that the U.S. government is investing in AI for mass surveillance, raising concerns about privacy and civil liberties. This highlights the potential misuse of AI technologies.

item.title: What Is Cursor AI? The AI Code Editor That Rewrote How Developers Work (2026)
item.description: Cursor AI, an AI code editor with over 2 million daily users and a $2 billion annual revenue, is transforming developer workflows. The article explains its unique features and target audience.

item.title: allratestoday-deepseek added to PyPI
item.description: The allratestoday-deepseek package has been added to PyPI, enabling real-time currency exchange rates integration into DeepSeek chat agents. This enhances the functionality of AI-driven applications.

item.title: allratestoday-deepseek 0.2.1
item.description: An update to allratestoday-deepseek (version 0.2.1) continues to support real-time currency exchange rates for DeepSeek chat agents, ensuring accurate financial data integration.

item.title: AirPods Pro 3 just dropped back down to one of the best prices in months at Amazon
item.description: Amazon has reduced the price of AirPods Pro 3 to one of the lowest in months, following a recent price increase. This offers consumers a chance to purchase at a better rate.

item.title: gemcode 0.3.113
item.description: Gemcode 0.3.113 has been released, featuring a local-first coding agent on Google Gemini and ADK. This aims to enhance coding efficiency and accessibility.

item.title: mnemostack 0.2.0a5
item.description: Mnemostack 0.2.0a5 introduces a memory stack for AI agents, integrating BM25, Qdrant, Memgraph, and a recall pipeline for enhanced inference. This development aims to improve AI agent performance and retrieval capabilities.

item.title: langchain-timbr 5.2.0
item.description: LangChain and LangGraph have released version 5.2.0, which allows parsing of LLM prompts into Timbr semantic SQL for execution. This update aims to streamline interactions with language models.

item.title: Your May 2026 Horoscope Includes Gemini Season & A Taurus New Moon
item.description: An astrologer discusses the May 2026 horoscope, highlighting the Gemini season and a Taurus new moon that could bring transformative changes. This forecast aims to guide individuals through upcoming astrological events.

item.title: ometa-diff added to PyPI
item.description: Ometa-diff has been added to PyPI, enhancing OpenMetadata with metadata version-diff intelligence. This addition aims to improve data management and tracking for developers.

item.title: Your Horoscope for the Week of April 26
item.description: This article offers a free weekly horoscope for readers, providing insights into astrological influences for the week. It aims to engage readers with personalized predictions.

item.title: Iran War: Israel Strikes Lebanon, Trump’s Negotiations Rug Pull, the View from the UAE
item.description: The article reports on Israel's military strikes in Lebanon amid ongoing tensions in the Iran War, alongside commentary on Trump's negotiations and media coverage. This highlights the complex geopolitical landscape.

item.title: azpaddypy 1.0.4
item.description: Azure's azpaddypy 1.0.4 SDK has been released, offering a range of cloud services including storage, AI projects, and document intelligence. This update aims to enhance developers' capabilities in building applications.

item.title: forge-harness added to PyPI
item.description: Forge-harness has been added to PyPI, providing a model-agnostic, self-learning agent harness with a Python SDK. This tool aims to facilitate the development of intelligent agents.

item.title: 3 Part Claude Framework That Completely Transforms Marketing Workflows
item.description: The article outlines a three-part Claude framework designed to transform marketing workflows through automation. It emphasizes the benefits of using Claude Design and Skills for efficiency.

item.title: sudden lag after servicing
item.description: A user reports experiencing sudden lag on their laptop after routine servicing, seeking help in a tech forum. This highlights common issues faced by users post-maintenance.
